title: Fix close fds of run --preserve-fds
body:
Test flakes mentioned in #6987 might be caused by uncorrect closing of file descriptor.
Remove the code to close file descriptors for `podman run` since it may close those used by other processes.

@giuseppe PTAL. The same flakes happened to podman exec once and got fixed, but does not work for `podman run`, is this a feasible fix?

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
If we remove this code, we end up keeping the fd open and possibly cause the other end of the pipe to hang indefinitely.

Are we making sure the fds are really opened in the tests when we specify `--preserve-fds=X`?  In bash it can be easily done with:

```
$ exec 3</dev/null
$ podman run --preserve-fds=1 ....
```

if we don't do that, then preserve-fds will pass down and close fd 3 opened by podman.

It's not the bash tests that are failing (AFAICT those have never flaked), it's the Ginkgo tests. And [that code](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8f7ed50cb20c04bbbb7c4907a183c07912d4bffb/test/e2e/run_test.go#L1042) really looks a lot like the one you fixed for the `exec` flake.

the unit test opens the file 
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/288ebec6e737c105fa0ef43412de4e0a8997feb9/test/e2e/run_test.go#L1043
LGTM, can we get the same fix for libpod/oci_conmon_exec_linux.go  ?

> LGTM, can we get the same fix for libpod/oci_conmon_exec_linux.go ?

Yes, will open a PR for that.

/lgtm
@QiWang19 Did you open the second PR?
